input.prompt: Add a support ticket classifier to this .NET 10 Web API. Tickets have a Subject (string), Description (string), Priority (int 1-3), and Category should be predicted as one of: Billing, Technical, General. Use the provided training data CSV. Show me the full implementation including model training, evaluation metrics, and a prediction endpoint.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the project. Let me build the full implementation:

1. Add ML.NET NuGet package
2. Create models (data classes for input/output)
3. Create model training service
4. Create prediction endpoint
5. Wire everything up in Program.cs

Let me plan the file structure:
- Models/TicketData.cs - input data model
- Models/TicketPrediction.cs - prediction output model
- Services/TicketModelTrainer.cs - training + evaluation
- Controllers/TicketClassifierController.cs - API endpoint
- Update Program.cs
- Update .csproj with ML.NET package


Now I'll start building out the implementation.

data.content: Server is up. Let me test both endpoints.

arguments.command: echo "=== METRICS ===" && curl -s http://localhost:5211/api/tickets/metrics | python3 -m json.tool && echo "" && echo "=== CLASSIFY (Technical) ===" && curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5211/api/tickets/classify -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"subject":"App crash","description":"The application crashes when I click submit","priority":1}' | python3 -m json.tool && echo "" && echo "=== CLASSIFY (Billing) ===" && curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5211/api/tickets/classify -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"subject":"Invoice issue","description":"I was overcharged on my last invoice","priority":2}' | python3 -m json.tool && echo "" && echo "=== CLASSIFY (General) ===" && curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5211/api/tickets/classify -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"subject":"How do I export data","description":"I want to know how to export my data to CSV","priority":3}' | python3 -m json.tool

data.content: All endpoints working — every test ticket classified correctly. Let me clean up.
data.reasoningText: All three predictions are correct (Technical, Billing, General). The confidence scores are a bit spread out which is expected with only 6 training samples, but the classifications are all correct. The metrics show perfect accuracy on the training set. Let me stop the server and summarize.

data.content:
Here's what was built:

**Files created/modified:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `TicketClassifier.csproj` | Added `Microsoft.ML 4.0.2` |
| `Models/TicketData.cs` | ML.NET data classes (`TicketData`, `TicketPrediction`) |
| `Models/TicketRequest.cs` | API DTOs with validation + metrics response |
| `Services/TicketModelService.cs` | Trains SDCA Maximum Entropy model, runs cross-validated evaluation, exposes prediction |
| `Controllers/TicketsController.cs` | `POST /api/tickets/classify` and `GET /api/tickets/metrics` |
| `Program.cs` | Registers model service as singleton |

**Pipeline:** Text featurization (Subject + Description) → concatenate with Priority → normalize → SDCA MaximumEntropy multiclass classifier.

**Endpoints:**
- `POST /api/tickets/classify` — accepts `{subject, description, priority}`, returns predicted category + per-class confidence scores
- `GET /api/tickets/metrics` — returns macro/micro accuracy, log loss, and per-class precision/recall/F1

> **Note:** The training set has only 6 rows, so confidence scores are spread thin even though classifications are correct. Add more rows to `Data/tickets.csv` for better real-world performance.
